observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and Management agreed to strictly require the OMA to:
Prioritize the review and analysis of prior year transactions to ultimately trace the causes of the difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts; and
Draw the necessary journal entries after the reconciliation and difference is established, to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal accounts of the different funds affected and furnish the AT of the corrections made therein. Henceforth, to constantly review and analyze the intra-agency transactions before these are recorded and ensure that these are simultaneously recognized between funds.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management committed to:
Require the OMA and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with accurate and fairly presented PPE accounts in the FS;
Avail the one-time cleansing of all PPE accounts as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability
Instruct the IC to conduct proper and complete physical count of all PPE of the MGC to determine the exact location, existence and actual condition of the PPE; and
Ensure the complete installation of the streetlights at Barangays Mauhang and Cabungahan that were already reported as part of the Other Infrastructure Assets. Otherwise, remove said assets from the PPE account and record the payments, if any, as receivables from concerned contractors and/or erring LGU officials.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:
Compel the OMA and the MTO to conduct a comprehensive and regular reconciliation of the MGC’s cash accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances on both records;
Strictly require the immediate investigation on the cause of the negative balance of the CLT account in the TF books and effect the necessary adjustments, if any; and
Require the MT to thoroughly review and correct the errors in the cashbooks and ensure the completeness and accuracy of the recorded collections and deposits prior to certifying the cashbook balance to uphold the integrity and reliability of the MGC’s financial reporting.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to require the OMA to prioritize the transfer of the cost of completed projects recorded as PPE under the TF books to the GF books thereafter, compute and record the appropriate AD to reflect the correct NBV of these assets, if warranted, otherwise, assessment of current cost is highly suggested.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the OMA to:
Refrain from recording purchases of supplies and materials as outright expense except those purchased out of the petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use, and ensure that all regular purchases shall be coursed through the inventory control account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place;
Ensure the correctness of entries and the posting of transactions in the books of accounts to present the correct amount in the FS; and
Record all the transactions of MGC in accordance with the prescribed guidelines in the IPSAS.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OMA to:
Confirm all outstanding balances of fund transfers under the Due to NGAs account and reconcile the latter with the SAs’ records;
Request formally from the SAs if the reconciled outstanding fund balances of completed PPAs could still be utilized by the MGC to finance other related PPAs. Otherwise, we recommend that Management direct the MA and the Municipal Treasurer to return immediately to the concerned SAs the unutilized fund balances; and
Revisit the prior year’s accounting entries pertaining to TF transactions on fund transfers, and identify the erroneous entries that caused the abnormal/negative balance. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
We further recommended and Management also agreed to instruct the OMA, together with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to:
Exert extra effort to identify the status of the PPAs that are still subject for verification; and
Maintain a detailed and complete schedules of fund transfers with supporting documents for easy monitoring and avoid general descriptions such as various or miscellaneous accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the OMA and MDRRMO to implement procedures that will ensure timely determination and transfer of unexpended QRF and MF – MOOE to the STF, thereafter, record the transfer to the TF books to present the correct balance of liability account as of year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that only those expenditures related to the implementation of DRRM programs, projects and activities should be charged against the LDRRMF. Also, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the MDRRMO to ensure the prompt distribution of the remaining relief goods to the identified beneficiaries to prevent the risk of spoilage, damage, or loss of the items and to ultimately fulfill the intended purpose of the relief effort; and
Strictly comply with its intended purpose of the QRF which is to provide immediate relief and support recovery activities in times of calamity.

We recommended and Management agreed to enforce immediate liquidation from the recipient Barangays of the financial assistance released to them, for which PPAs have already been completed and funds utilized in compliance with the requisites provided in COA Circular 2012-001 and the MOA.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to remind the OMA to:
Facilitate the timely recording and set-up of taxes withheld between the GF, SEF and TF at year-end to immediately identify those that were not yet taken up in the books and exercise extra diligence to trace the cause of the abnormal monthly balances for each SL and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted;
Ensure that the correct and accurate amounts are transferred to the GF from the SEF and TF and reconciled with the actual taxes withheld to avoid the incurrence of abnormal balance between the said funds; and
Require the prompt and complete remittance of taxes withheld for the period to avoid the incurrence and accumulation of unremitted taxes by the end of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Trace the remaining unidentified unremitted amount, and afterwards, facilitate the remittance of the same to the GSIS, if warranted; and
Exhaust all efforts to locate the relevant documents to support the accounts with abnormal balances, and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments in the books, if necessary.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to instruct the OMA to prioritize the reconciliation of its records with those of the Pag-IBIG to identify the nature and specific details and validity of the recorded amount. Thereafter, prepare the necessary supporting documents to settle the outstanding balance or facilitate the pertinent adjustments in the books, if warranted.
We also recommended and the OMA agreed to investigate the cause of the abnormal balance and effect the necessary adjustment, if warranted to arrive at the correct account balance.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the OMA to reconcile their records with the payroll and remittance officers to identify the nature and other details of the unremitted amount. Thereafter, prepare the necessary supporting documents to settle the outstanding balance or facilitate the pertinent adjustments in the books, if warranted.
We also recommended and Management likewise agreed to exert extra effort to gather all the documents related to the NHIP to determine whether there is an existing obligation to the PhilHealth ROV and eventually settle any unreconciled balance with the agency.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation with modifications and Management committed to:
Require the GFPS to fully comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2021-008 by using the created and assigned responsibility center codes as additional reference in its GPB and GAD AR;
Remind the OMA to ensure correct and reliable recording in the SLs for the GFPS and other GAD-related financial transactions and that only those activities that address specific gender issues and are covered in the GPB and GAD AR are recorded in the GAD SLs; and
Instruct the MBO to indicate the RCs in the GAD Monitoring and Utilization Report to facilitate ease of reconciliation and ensure a more integrated and comprehensive accounting, monitoring, and reporting of the GAD programs and funds.

a. Prioritize the review and analysis of prior year transactions to ultimately trace the causes of the difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts; and
b. Draw the necessary journal entries after the reconciliation and difference is established, to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal accounts of the different funds affected and furnish the AT of the corrections made therein. Henceforth, to constantly review and analyze the intra-agency transactions before these are recorded and ensure that these are simultaneously recognized between funds.

Remind the OMA to ensure correct and reliable recording in the SLs for the GFPS and other GAD-related financial transactions and that only those activities that address specific gender issues and are covered in the GPB and GAD AR are recorded in the GAD SLs; The OMA will ensure that only transactions covered under the GPB and GAD AR are recorded in the GAD SLs and that records are updated and properly maintained.
c. Instruct the MBO to indicate the RCs in the GAD Monitoring and Utilization Report to facilitate ease of reconciliation and ensure a more integrated and comprehensive accounting, monitoring, and reporting of the GAD programs and funds. The MBO will include responsibility center codes in the GAD Monitoring and Utilization Report to enhance reconciliation and accuracy of reporting.
observations.reason: The GFPS incorporated responsibility center codes in both the GPB and GAD AR, ensuring compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008. The OMA completed reconciliation and ensured accurate recording of GAD-related transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly require the OMA to:
a. Trace the causes of the difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts; and
b. After the reconciliation and difference is established, draw the necessary journal entry voucher to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal accounts of the affected funds and furnish the AT of the corrections made therein. Henceforth, to constantly review and analyze the intra-agency transactions before these are recorded and ensure that these are simultaneously recognized between funds.
observations.managementAction:
a. Trace the causes of the difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts; The OMA will trace the causes of the unreconciled difference and prepare the reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts. Afterward, the OMA will draw the necessary reconciliation report.
b. After the reconciliation and difference is established, draw the necessary journal entry voucher to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal accounts of the affected funds and furnish the AT of the corrections made therein. Thereafter, post necessary adjusting entries and implement a regular review process to ensure intra-agency transactions are accurately and simultaneously recorded between funds, with ongoing monthly reviews to prevent future discrepancies.
observations.reason: Insufficient personnel available to prioritize the reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds. Ongoing reconciliation.

observations.managementAction:
1. Require the OMA and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to come up with accurate and fairly presented PPE accounts in the FS; The OMA and the Inventory Committee will reconcile their records and reports on PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 to ensure accurate and fairly presented PPE balances in the financial statements.
2. Avail the one-time cleansing of all PPE accounts as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; The Inventory Committee will initiate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 as prerequisites for PPE cleansing.
3. Instruct the IC to conduct proper and complete physical count of all PPE of the MGC to determine the exact location, existence and actual condition of the PPE; The Inventory Committee will conduct a thorough physical count of all PPE to determine the existence and actual condition of the PPE. The Inventory Committee has conducted a physical counting of assets; final report will be submitted upon completion of reconciliation with books.
4. Ensure the complete installation of the streetlights at Barangays Mauhang and Cabungahan that were already reported as part of the Other Infrastructure Assets. Otherwise, remove said assets from the PPE account and record the payments, if any, as receivables from concerned contractors and/or erring LGU officials. The OMA will ensure the complete installation of streetlights at Barangays Mauhang and Cabungahan; if necessary accounting adjustments will be made once confirmation is received.
observations.reason: Gathering and validating of data from various offices and departments. Coordination between the OMA and Inventory Committee has started; reconciliation of PPE balances is ongoing to align reports and ensure accuracy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Require the MGSO/Property Officer and the OMA to prepare and maintain the SCs and SLCs, respectively, and conduct periodic reconciliation of both records in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
2. Consider measures to facilitate the possibility of converting an RPTAR-subsidiary ledger; hiring of additional personnel to do the manual task if automation is not probable.
3. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts after reconciliation with the MTO and furnish the AT with copies of the JEV with complete supporting documents;
observations.managementAction:
1. Require the MGSO/Property Officer and the OMA to prepare and maintain the SCs and SLCs, respectively, and conduct periodic reconciliation of both records in accordance with the existing rules and regulations; The MGSO/Property Officer and OMA will prepare and maintain the Stock Cards and SLCs, respectively, and conduct periodic reconciliation in line with existing rules and regulations.
2. Consider measures to facilitate the possibility of converting an RPTAR-subsidiary ledger; hiring of additional personnel to do the manual task if automation is not probable; The proposed measure is to explore procurement of a system for automation of the taxpayer’s record that can be linked to accounting records or hiring of additional personnel to manage the monitoring and reporting.
3. Prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts after reconciliation with the MTO and furnish the AT with copies of the JEV with complete supporting documents; The OMA will prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts after reconciliation and submit supporting JEVs to the AT for review.
observations.reason: Due to the high volume of transactions and a shortage of manpower to manage the monitoring and preparation of SCs and SLCs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the OMA to:
1. Conduct retrieval of all documents pertaining to prior years’ records in order to determine the nature and composition of the book reconciling items;
2. Take up all valid reconciling items by preparing journal entry vouchers duly supported with proper documentation and furnish the AT with copies of the same; and
3. Prioritize the retrieval of documents and recording of the identified book reconciling items for the 14 bank accounts that were already closed to finally reflect the correct CIB balances at yearend.
observations.managementAction:
1. Conduct retrieval of all documents pertaining to prior years’ records in order to determine the nature and composition of the book reconciling items; Review and analyze prior years’ records to identify the causes and nature of long-standing reconciling items.
2. Take up all valid reconciling items by preparing journal entry vouchers duly supported with proper documentation and furnish the AT with copies of the same; Record necessary adjustments in the books once prior year supporting documents are retrieved and submit the JEVs and vouchers to COA.
3. Prioritize the retrieval of documents and recording of the identified book reconciling items for the 14 bank accounts that were already closed to finally reflect the correct CIB balances at yearend. Validate and record adjustments for previously closed bank accounts to ensure that only active and valid balances are reflected in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Difficulty locating old documents and reviewing archived incomplete records from previous years.

observations.managementAction:
1. Strictly require the OMA and the MTO to conduct a comprehensive and regular reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances on both records; Conduct joint and periodic reconciliation between the OMA and the MTO to verify and align the balances of cash accounts, and prepare the necessary adjustments to ensure accuracy of both records.
2. Require the immediate investigation on the cause of the negative balance of the CLT account in the TF books and effect the necessary adjustments, if any; Examine and determine the cause of the negative balance in the Trust Fund CLT account and make the necessary correcting entries once verified.
observations.reason: Discrepancy in prior period records and incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Advise the LFC and other officials concerned to regularly monitor the MGC’s cash position and immediately notify the LCE of any cash insufficiency noted; and
2. Require the OMA and the MTO to ensure that monies held in trust are used solely for their intended purpose/s and that all disbursements are made solely for their intended purposes;
3. Review proposed expenditures to confirm necessity and compliance with approved programs, ensuring that only essential and urgent expenses are prioritized.
observations.managementAction:
1. Advise the LFC and other officials concerned to regularly monitor the MGC’s cash position and immediately notify the LCE of any cash insufficiency noted; Strengthen LFC coordination to regularly review and monitor the LGU’s cash position and promptly inform the LCE of any shortage or deficit.
2. Require the OMA and the MTO to ensure that monies held in trust are used solely for their intended purpose/s; Implement strict monitoring and documentation of trust fund transactions to ensure that all disbursements are made solely for their intended purposes.
3. Review proposed expenditures to confirm necessity and compliance with approved programs, ensuring that only essential and urgent expenses are prioritized; Review proposed expenditures to confirm necessity and compliance and prioritize essential and urgent expenses.
observations.reason: Challenges in retrieving supporting documents for the incorrect recording of transactions from previous years, resulting in an unreliable balance in the Trust Liabilities account.

observations.managementAction:
1. Make the necessary reconciliation and evaluation of the unidentified balances and ensure that proper disclosures and adjustments shall be drawn to address the same; Conduct a thorough reconciliation and evaluation of unidentified balances and ensure proper adjustments are made to address discrepancies.
2. Consider the filing of request for write-off of those long outstanding receivables where settlement could no longer be ascertained in strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-008. The OMA will request for write-off following the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 for dormant or non-moving balances.
observations.reason: Records from previous years are unavailable, making it difficult to determine details and pursue collection. Due from LGUs account amounting to ₱5,300,000.00 pertaining to Barangay Development Programs has been fully liquidated except for ₱250,000.00 granted to Brgy. Timbayog.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:
1. The MAO to secure and submit to COA, MTO and OMA the master list of farmer beneficiaries who availed of the fertilizer loan since 2016 as well as copies of the contracts/MOA to support the same;
2. The OMA to exert extra effort in the retrieval of detailed information and documents on said loans receivables and draw the necessary adjusting entries to record such Receivables under the GF, if warranted; and
3. The MTO to send demand letters and intensify efforts to require the principal borrowers as well as the co-makers, if any, to pay the unpaid balances of their loans. In case of continuous default in payment of loans, seek assistance for the enforcement of legal remedies in the collection of long outstanding receivables and for the filing of appropriate charges to the erring officers and delinquent borrowers.
observations.managementAction:
1. The MAO to secure and submit to COA, MTO and OMA the master list of farmer beneficiaries who availed of the fertilizer loan since 2016 as well as copies of the contracts/MOA to support the same; The MAO will secure and submit to COA, MTO, and OMA the master list of farmer beneficiaries who availed of the fertilizer loan since 2016 along with copies of contracts/MOAs.
2. The OMA to exert extra effort in the retrieval of detailed information and documents on said loans receivables and draw the necessary adjusting entries to record such Receivables under the GF, if warranted; The OMA will retrieve detailed documents related to the loans receivable and draw the necessary adjusting entries to record these receivables under the General Fund if warranted.
3. The MTO to send demand letters and intensify efforts to require the principal borrowers as well as the co-makers, if any, to pay the unpaid balances of their loans. In case of continuous default in payment of loans, seek assistance for the enforcement of legal remedies in the collection of long outstanding receivables and for the filing of appropriate charges to the erring officers and delinquent borrowers. The MTO will re-issue demand letters and intensify collection efforts for unpaid loan balances from principal borrowers and co-makers; if continued default persists, the MTO will seek legal remedies.
observations.reason: The contracts/MOA were submitted to COA but were not accepted due to volume of items. The master list were submitted to MTO and OMA. Demand letters were sent out; collection remains challenging as many borrowers unwilling to pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the OMA to prioritize the transfer of the cost of completed projects recorded as PPE under the TF books to the GF books thereafter, compute and record the appropriate AD to reflect the correct NBV of these assets, if warranted, otherwise, assessment of current cost is highly suggested.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will prioritize the transfer of the cost of completed projects recorded as PPE under the TF books to the GF books, verify their completion and validity, and after verification facilitate the necessary journal entries for transfer to General Fund, starting with those supported by complete documentation.
observations.reason: Lack of complete documentation for the list of completed projects under the Trust Fund and preparing the necessary journal documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure thorough evaluation of proposed priority development projects whether funded by current or continuing appropriations to achieve socio-economic development outcomes and maximize benefits for the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management will ensure thorough evaluation of proposed priority development projects whether funded by current or continuing appropriations to implement and maximize benefits for the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The 20% Development Fund recorded a utilization rate of 93.13% as of December 31, 2024. Unutilized balances from December 2023 were re-aligned or subsequently used for implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OMA and various offices in-charge to obtain copies of the final and signed MOA, as basis for the utilization of these fund transfers and furnish the AT for proper evaluation of the implementation and propriety of the disbursements made in relation to these TF accounts, in consonance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Require the BAC to obtain missing bidding documents and Ensure the OMA and concerned offices to obtain copies of the final and signed MOA as basis for the utilization of fund transfers and furnish the AT for proper evaluation of implementation and propriety of disbursements made in relation to these TF accounts.
observations.reason: Some of the MOAs from previous years lack signatures while others are no longer available. The concerned offices have been requested to coordinate with the SAs for copies of signed MOAs.

observations.managementAction: Management will ensure the full utilization and prompt implementation of the remaining PPAs in the following year and instruct the OMA to initiate the return or refund of unutilized balances to the Source Agencies for fully implemented PPAs in line with COA Circular No. 94-013. The OMA will reconcile confirmation results with source agencies and prepare necessary adjustments in the books upon completion of supporting documents, and submit liquidation reports to source agencies. For dormant balances, the OMA will request write-off following COA procedures.
observations.reason: Undocumented and insufficient details of transactions; various prior years’ documents being retrieved or denied for write-off (e.g., NMIS request denied). Some unutilized balances from DAR and DILG were returned to source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OMA to reclassify and conduct an extensive analysis on the recorded unidentified SEF balance under GF in order to determine its details or exact nature. Also, review the negative or abnormal balances and trace the erroneous entries made in the books to immediately facilitate the adjusting entries needed to arrive at the correct balances.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the OMA to reclassify and conduct extensive analysis on the unidentified SEF balance under GF to determine its exact nature. The OMA will conduct an extensive analysis of the originating balances and trace erroneous entries to facilitate necessary adjustments and arrive at the correct balances.
observations.reason: The unavailability of records prior to 2013 affected the verification of the originating balances. Adjustments have been made to correct the negative balances in the Due to LGU account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OMA to secure a valid waiver or authority directly from the individual officer or employees concerned to establish the authority and substantiate the transactions previously written off; and thereafter, make the necessary adjustments in the books where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Secure a valid waiver or authority directly from the individual officers concerned to establish the authority and substantiate the transactions previously written off; The OMA reversed the previous write-off of the Due to Officers and Employees account upon securing the required waivers/authorities and returned the amounts to the concerned employees.
observations.reason: The valid claims of the concerned employees have been duly recognized and returned to them.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Prioritize the application of the 10-year SWMP to NSWMC and address all requirements requested by the EMB-ROV and NSWMC; and
2. Ensure strict observance of the law relative to the SWM by firmly enforcing the penalties as provided in RA No. 9003 and its IRR to ensure compliance with solid waste management regulations.
observations.managementAction:
1. Prioritize the application of the 10-year SWMP to NSWMC and address all requirements requested by the EMB-ROV and NSWMC; The MENRO will prioritize the approval process of the 10-Year SWMP and address requirements requested by EMB-ROV and NSWMC. Finalization of the Waste Analysis and Characterization Study is being undertaken to update and complete the plan for submission to NSWMC.
2. Ensure strict observance of the law relative to the SWM by firmly enforcing the penalties as provided in RA No. 9003 and its IRR to ensure compliance with solid waste management regulations; The MENRO will enforce penalties outlined in RA No. 9003 and IRR to ensure compliance. Due to lack of IRR to enable collection of penalties and fees, the concerned departments will formulate the Implementing Rules and Regulations next year to facilitate enforcement of penalty collections.
observations.reason: Finalizing the Waste Analysis and Characterization Study. The 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan is targeted to be finalized and submitted to NSWMC. Enforcement of penalties pending IRR and preparation of Implementing Rules and Regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management remind the OMA to facilitate the timely recording and set-up of taxes withheld between the GF, SEF and TF at year-end to immediately identify those that were not yet taken up in the books and exercise extra diligence to trace the cause of the abnormal monthly balances for each SL and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will ensure timely recording and set-up of taxes withheld between GF, SEF and TF and validate abnormal balances per subsidiary ledger, prepare adjusting entries if necessary.
observations.reason: The reconciled differences were adjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Remit outstanding contributions and loan amortizations immediately to the GSIS to avoid penalties; and
2. Exert extra effort to trace the unidentified unremitted amount of ₱173,576.31, and afterwards, facilitate the remittance of the same to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction:
1. Remit outstanding contributions and loan amortizations immediately to the GSIS; The OMA will prioritize remittance of outstanding GSIS contributions and loan amortizations and ensure timely remittance of monthly dues to prevent penalties and surcharges.
2. Exert extra effort to trace the unidentified unremitted amount of ₱173,576.31 and thereafter remit once confirmed; The OMA will trace the unidentified unremitted amount and, if confirmed, facilitate its remittance to GSIS.
observations.reason: Awaiting response and confirmation from GSIS for the request for waiver and verification. Limited personnel to handle reconciliation tasks; current year contributions are remitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OMA to reconcile their records with the payroll and remittance officers to identify the nature and other details of the unremitted amount; thereafter, prepare the necessary supporting documents to settle the outstanding balance or facilitate the pertinent adjustments in the books, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will reconcile Pag-IBIG records with payroll and remittance officers to identify unremitted amounts and prepare the necessary supporting documents to settle outstanding balances or make adjustments as warranted. The OMA will investigate the cause of the abnormal MP2 account balance and prepare adjusting entries once validated.
observations.reason: Reconciliation and validation of prior transactions still in progress; negative balance has been adjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OMA to reconcile their records with the payroll and remittance officers to identify the nature and other details of the unremitted amount. Thereafter, prepare the necessary supporting documents to settle the outstanding balance or facilitate the pertinent adjustments in the books, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will reconcile their PhilHealth contribution records with payroll and remittance officers to identify and validate unremitted amounts and prepare necessary supporting documents to settle outstanding balances or adjust books if warranted. The OMA will also exert extra effort to gather all documents related to NHIP obligations and allocate budget for settlement where possible.
observations.reason: The account includes P642,650.00 that pertains to Due to Philhealth for Indigent Families that has long been reflected in the Subsidiary Ledger without available supporting documents. As of September 30, 2025, the outstanding balance in the books represents the long-outstanding amount of P642,650.00 and the current year’s contributions were remitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all insurable properties of the MGC are insured with the GIF to provide reasonable protection against any damage to, or loss of properties due to fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will ensure that all insurable properties of the MGC are insured with the GIF to provide reasonable protection against loss due to fortuitous events. The Municipal Accountant will coordinate with concerned offices to identify insurable properties and prioritize insurance coverage, and management will allocate funds in future budgets to cover insurance premiums.
observations.reason: Insufficient funds to insure all properties. Management will allocate additional funds next year to cover insurable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MA and OIC of the MTO to regularly reconcile the RPT and SET Receivable accounts and consider measures to facilitate the maintenance of subsidiary records for a large number of taxpayers, such as procurement of a system for automation or hiring additional personnel; and prepare necessary adjustments after reconciliation and furnish the AT with copies of JEVs with complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will regularly reconcile RPT and SET receivable accounts with the MTO, propose procurement of automated system or hire additional personnel for maintenance of subsidiary records, and prepare necessary adjustments in the books after reconciliation and submit JEVs with supporting documents to the AT.
observations.reason: Advance payments were included in RPT and SET receivable lists historically; adjustments for current year balances have been made. Maintenance of RPTAR-subsidiary ledger is tedious and the OMA is proposing system procurement or hiring additional personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Require the MA and OIC of the MEO to reconcile records on CIP and submit promptly the necessary SDs as basis for adjustments in the CIP accounts;
2. Instruct the IC to conduct proper and complete physical count of all PPE; and
3. Require the MEO to submit status report of completed projects to the OMA for timely recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The OMA and Inventory Committee will reconcile records and the MEO will provide necessary supporting documents. The Inventory Committee will ensure due diligence in the conduct of physical counts and the MEO-OIC will submit status reports of completed projects to OMA.
observations.reason: Discrepancies arise from prior years’ accounting records; ongoing physical counts and reconciliation. Some prior year supporting documents are not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OMA to conduct retrieval of prior years’ records to determine the nature of the book reconciling items; take up all valid reconciling items by preparing JEVs with proper documentation and furnish the AT with copies; and prioritize retrieval and recording of reconciling items for the 14 closed bank accounts to reflect correct CIB balances.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will retrieve prior years’ records to determine the nature and composition of reconciling items, record necessary adjustments in the books as supporting documents are retrieved, and prioritize retrieval for the 14 closed bank accounts to ensure only active and valid balances are reflected in financial statements.
observations.reason: Records and documents related to prior year transactions cannot be fully retrieved due to multiple office relocations and other causes; OMA continues efforts to retrieve and record these items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OMA to evaluate thoroughly the accounts with negative balances and thereafter prepare the necessary adjustments in the books and submit required liquidation documents to the SAs to minimize or eliminate the unreconciled balances of fund transfers; request authority from COA to write-off dormant transfers following COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will reconcile confirmation results with source agencies and prepare necessary adjustments in the books; submit required liquidation documents to SAs; and request authority from COA to write-off dormant fund transfers following COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: Unavailability of prior year supporting documents; request for write-off of a dormant account submitted to Audit Team and pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC of the MTO and other officials concerned to facilitate the hiring of needed personnel in the MAO and MTO to address lack of manpower; and take appropriate action to address matters pertaining to organization and management; personnel administration; position classification and pay plan; staffing patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Facilitate the hiring of needed personnel in the MAO and MTO to address lack of manpower and improve collection and enforcement of Real Property Tax and SET. Concerned offices will process recruitment of additional staff and pursue organizational measures including legislative actions for creation and filling of positions.
observations.reason: Request for additional personnel relayed; hiring expected to be implemented by middle of next year. Delay in creating and filling positions; budgetary constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filling up of vacant plantilla positions and resort to hiring of casual or emergency personnel in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 40, series of 1998 and Section 211 (b) of the GAAM, Volume I, to avoid hiring significant number of JO personnel; and the SB to take appropriate action to address matters pertaining to organization and management; personnel administration; position classification and pay plan; staffing patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Prioritize filling up vacant plantilla positions and resort to hiring casuals or emergency personnel in accordance with Civil Service rules; and the SB to take appropriate legislative action to address organization and staffing matters including creation of plantilla positions as needed.
observations.reason: Limited availability of funds to hire casuals. Majority of vacant plantilla positions have been filled by end of year; as of Sept 30, 2025 there are only 140 JOs hired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MEO submit immediately the necessary supporting documents as basis for adjustments in the CIP accounts; and that the OMA reconstruct the details of the CIP accounts and effect adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: Require the ME to submit the necessary supporting documents for projects under CIP to the OMA for recording and adjustments. The MEO-OIC will provide necessary documents and assist OMA in reconstructing CIP details for necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Supporting documents for prior years’ projects are being retrieved. MGC will avail one-time cleansing of PPE in 2026.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare the indicative and supplemental APP based on PPMPs and submit the same to GPPB and COA in accordance with Section 7.4 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat will prepare the indicative and supplemental APP based on PPMPs and submit the same to GPPB and COA as required. BAC to ensure PPMPs are prepared and submitted during budget and procurement planning.
observations.reason: Non-submission of indicative and supplemental APP to GPPB; PPMPs prepared by the BAC Secretariat pending submission to GPPB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert utmost effort in retrieving prior years’ records and prior year supporting documents to gather complete or sufficient information to support the said dormant accounts and request for write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005, if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will continue efforts to retrieve prior years’ records and supporting documents to gather complete information to support dormant accounts and prepare request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 where applicable.
observations.reason: Unavailability of prior years’ records. Transactions with available supporting documents were paid and adjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the MA to continue and strengthen coordination with the ME and the MGSO-ICO to determine the condition of the infrastructure assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Infrastructure Assets account balances in the FS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will continue and enhance coordination with the ME and MGSO-ICO to determine the condition of infrastructure assets constructed in 2015 and earlier and evaluate carrying amounts for necessary adjustments in the books. The MEO and MGSO to conduct inventories and validations in preparation for one-time PPE cleansing.
observations.reason: Ongoing reconciliation of books and physical count. In preparation for the one-time PPE cleansing, carrying amounts will be evaluated and adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a justification why the irregular expenditures of ₱445,068 shall not be disallowed in audit and require the accountable officers to refund the travel expense.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted justification and instructed the accountable officers to refund the travel expense of ₱445,068.00 to the MTO. The refund was made.
observations.reason: The refund has been made last May 16, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the MA–ICO to take up the unrecorded disbursements by preparing JEVs duly supported with the DVs and their SDs and submit the JEVs and vouchers to COA for audit and verification; and reconstruct the details of unrecorded disbursements and submit the JEVs and vouchers to COA for audit and verification.
observations.managementAction: The OMA will retrieve SDs and supporting documents and prepare JEVs for remaining unrecorded disbursements which pertain to prior years; the Municipal Accountant will reconstruct details of unrecorded disbursements and submit JEVs and vouchers to COA.
observations.reason: Supporting documents for remaining unrecorded check disbursements pertaining to prior years cannot be retrieved; amount reduced to ₱7,363,905.04 as of Dec 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the MT and the MA–ICO to trace the whereabouts of stale/cancelled checks and thereafter, prepare the necessary adjustments in their respective records duly supported with complete SDs and reconstruct the details of stale/cancelled checks and submit the JEVs, checks and SDs to COA for audit and verification.
observations.managementAction: The MA–ICO and Municipal Treasurer will trace stale/cancelled checks and prepare necessary adjustments in the records once supporting documents are retrieved; reconstruct details of stale/cancelled checks and submit JEVs and documents to COA.
observations.reason: Supporting documents involving prior years are being retrieved; some checks reversed in accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit the lacking eligibility and other bidding documents to COA and address compliance with RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat to compile missing eligibility and bidding documents and furnish to COA for verification; suppliers asked to submit required eligibility documents.
observations.reason: Closure of the supplier’s business hindered obtaining the bidding documents.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant (MA) and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to come up with accurate and fairly presented PPE accounts in the FS.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to:
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and
Require the MA and the Municipal Engineer (ME) to submit immediately the required records and documents that will support the existence, ownership and condition of the unidentifiable PPE items.
We also recommended and the MA agreed to make the necessary adjustments to record properly the PPE items in the books of the MGC in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and submit the journal entry vouchers (JEV) together with complete supporting documents (SD) to the AT for verification.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the ME to submit immediately the necessary SDs as basis of the OMA for the adjustments in the CIP accounts; and
Ensure the prompt submission of complete and necessary SDs for all projects to the OMA by the concerned offices in order to come up with accurate and reliable CIP account balances.
We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed to require the MA and the ME to reconcile their records pertaining to the projects recorded under the CIP accounts in order to reflect these accurately and reliably in the FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Enjoin the LDRRM Office to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities; and
Monitor closely the implementation of all PPAs under the LDRRMF to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.
We also reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the MT to facilitate the immediate transfer of CYs 2017 to 2020 unspent DRRMF from the GF to the STF and thereafter, require the MA to properly take up the transfer in the books of the MGC.
We likewise recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the utilization of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF that were transferred to the STF to ensure that these funds are used for the intended purpose within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the BAC, the Secretariat and the Technical Working Group to abide strictly by the standards set forth by RA No. 9184, its 2016 Revised IRR and other relevant issuances on government procurement;
Submit within five working days upon execution or issuance of all copies of perfected contracts together with the SDs as required under COA Circular 2009-001; and
Instruct the MA and the ME to observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 on the procurement/implementation of infrastructure projects at all times to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the reporting requirements of PPAs funded from disaster relief aid to achieve transparency and accountability in the use of the fund.

1.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a)
BAC to (i) explain why procurements from online shopping sites were
allowed and cite its legal basis; and (ii) see to it that the procurement
processes and documentation required by R.A. No. 9184 are complied; and (b)
Municipal Accountant (i) submit the lacking documents for the payments made in relation to COVID-19; and (ii) ensure that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are complete before processing for payment.

2.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) reconcile their respective
Cash-in-Bank records with the depository banks within ten days from the
receipt of bank statements and with the Treasurer’s cash records at least
on quarterly basis; (b) continue its efforts in resolving the disparity
between bank and book records, and effect adjustments of any identified
reconciling items to show correct balances in the financial statements; and
(c) make proper disposition of the dormant and unreported bank
accounts.

3.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the:
(a) Inventory Committee and MGSO to (i) ensure that the PPE items are
properly labelled either before its issuance to end-user or during the
conduct of inventory count; (ii) complete the conduct of physical
inventory count and reconcile its result with both accounting and
property records; (iii) submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team;
(b) MGSO to (i) properly instruct the designated Property Officers of their
duties relative to the said designation; and (ii) prepare and maintain
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for each PPE item; and
(c) Accounting Office to (i) continue exerting efforts to trace back and
substantiate the old balances of the PPE accounts in order to provide
the correct depreciation for each type of properties; (ii) prepare and
maintain PPELCs for each PPE item; and (iii) maintain and update
ADS as supporting schedule for Depreciation Cost.

4.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MPDO Head and
OIC-Municipal Engineer to (i) judiciously plan for development projects
with the end view of timely execution of development projects; (ii) ensure
that these development projects are procurement-and-implementation-
ready and free from any encumbrances that may cause delay of public
service; and (iii) ensure that the entirety of 20% DF or any other
government fund for that matter, are optimally used; and (b) Field or
Project Engineers for the continuous monitoring of infrastructure
projects, aimed at making timely interventions in case of lapses in
implementation.

5.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MDRRM Officer
to refrain from charging and utilizing the MDRRM fund aside from those
embodied in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations; (b)
Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain that all disbursements from the
Special Trust Fund is properly earmarked and recorded in pertinent
registries to ensure that such disbursements have appropriations; (c)
Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the documents required to support the
regularity of the payments for OT Pay and Risk Allowance; (ii) maintain
subsidiary ledgers for the STF per appropriate year of transfer; (iii)
ensure proper classification and recording of transactions; and (iv)
determine the actual Welfare Goods actually distributed, and make the
necessary adjusting entries and properly account for inventories not yet
distributed; and (d) Property Officer to properly maintain stock cards of
the welfare goods maintained by the Municipality for proper monitoring
of inventories.

6.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to advise the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) devise measures on how to
ensure continuity of office work amidst the restrictions of the COVID-19
pandemic; and (b) henceforth continue to work on the timely submission
to the Office of the Auditor of the Municipality’s accounts and financial
reports.

7.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) direct the Accounting personnel to update regularly the
Subsidiary Ledger of Cash Advances; (b) monitor and remind AOs to
immediately liquidate their outstanding CAs as soon as the purpose has
already been served; (c) refrain the practice of granting additional CAs to
AOs with previous CAs not yet liquidated /settled; and (d) make sure that
all Municipal employees with outstanding CAs who are about to resign,
retire, or transfer to other agencies to settle their CA before granting them
the necessary clearance.

9.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GAD Focal System
to (a) facilitate the updating of GAD database; (b) coordinate with the
Philippine Statistics Office for the updating of the CBMS; (c) utilize the
allocated minimum requirement and make use of attributions for other
PPAs; and (d) comply with the provisions of the aforementioned circular
on using the tools and mechanism to properly attribute the cost of PPA’s
identified for more gender-responsive GPB and Accomplishment Report.

10.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) continue its compliance with RMC No. 23-2007 and BIR
TRR No. 10-2008; and (b) exert effort to verify the balances of reconciling
items to support its validity and take appropriate action to completely
account for the balances.

11.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously deduct and remit
the GSIS contributions and loan amortizations in accordance with the
GSIS law.

12.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) continuously comply with the National Health Insurance
Act by remitting all contributions to the PhilHealth; and (b) work on
retrieving records pertinent to the unreconciled amount and adjust the
balance of the account, if necessary.

Accountant to (a) continuously comply with R.A. No. 9679 by remitting all
contributions withheld to the Home Development Mutual Fnnd and (b)
work on retrieving records pertinent to the unreconciled amount and
adjust the balance of the account, as warranted.

responsible/ liable to immediately settle the suspensions in the total
amount of ₱4,125,500.00.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee to reconcile their records and reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 in order to come up with accurate and fairly presented PPE accounts in the FS.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to properly record the PPE items in the books of the Municipality in accordance with the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000 provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Further, we recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to exercise due diligence in the conduct of the physical count of PPE and in the preparation of RPCPPE in order to come up with accurate and reliable reports.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to prepare and maintain complete and accurate records of all LGU-funded projects in order to provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with a reliable basis in recording the CIP accounts in the books of accounts.
We also recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records pertaining to the projects recorded under the CIP account in order to reflect them accurately and reliably in the FS.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect the proposed adjustments and furnish the Audit Team with copies of JEVs together with complete supporting documents of all adjustments made in the books affecting the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts; and
Review and analyze transactions thoroughly before taking up the transactions in the books to prevent recording errors and variances between the reciprocal accounts.
We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts in order to come up with accurate balances presented in the FS.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to indicate the specific purpose of fund transfers in the authorities to debit or credit accounts to determine the propriety and facilitate proper accounting of the same.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Refrain from recording supplies and materials expenses without the required documentation;
Prepare JEVs based on transaction documents presented in accordance with Section 31 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and
Carefully analyze the transactions before taking them up in the books of accounts in order to properly account for the acquisition and issuance of the supplies and materials inventory based on IPSAS 12, Inventories and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.
We also recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Inventory Committee to exercise diligence in performing its functions and responsibilities as required in the pertinent provisions of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Require the Inventory Committee and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation between the Inventories accounts balances per books and per RPCI; and
Require the MGSO and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to properly prepare and maintain complete and updated SCs and SLCs, respectively, and reconcile their records regularly.
Further, we recommended and the MGSO agreed to:
Prepare and use the RIS and SSMI in accordance with Sections 41 and 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Annex 33 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and
Come up with internal control procedures that will ensure proper handling of inventories to prevent or minimize the risk of loss and misuse such as maintenance of logbook for expected and actual deliveries based on approved POs and delivery receipts and making use of ICS for issuance of small tangible items with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE.
Furthermore, we recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to prepare the indicative and supplemental APP based on PPMPs and supplemental PPMPs, respectively, and submit the same to the GPPB and to COA in accordance with Section 7.4 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Submit the necessary supporting documents in order to provide sufficient basis in taking up the adjustments in the books amounting to ₱2.01 million;
Prepare and maintain the JEVs and the Abstract of RPT Collections in accordance with Annex 23 and Section 22 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, respectively; and
Observe due diligence in recording transactions by using the proper accounts as provided under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and ensuring sufficient basis and documentation in order to come up with accurate and faithfully represented FS.
We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Exclude the advance payments received from taxpayers in the preparation of the Certified List of Taxpayers in order to provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant an accurate basis for the set-up of RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
Submit the Certified List of Delinquent Taxpayers as of December 31, 2020, to the SB to request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection of RPT and SET.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records in order to come up with accurate and reliable reports on the levy of RPT and SET.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Exert utmost effort in retrieving prior years’ records and documents to gather complete or sufficient information to support the accuracy and validity of the accounts balances presented in the FS and facilitate the collection, payment or other appropriate action;
Request for the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005, if applicable; and
Consider the reversion of accounts payable which remain outstanding for at least two years, for which no actual administrative or judicial claim has been filed.
Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare and maintain complete and updated SLs and other subsidiary records of the accounts presented in the FS in accordance with Section 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and
Observe proper recording and classification of accounts in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to come up with accurate and fairly presented FS.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation and Management agreed to strictly comply with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1, by taking into consideration the following:
Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the PPA, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.;
Adopt strategies to maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a desired quality of life for all; and
Ensure that the 20 per cent Development Fund is used solely for development PPAs and no amount is utilized for expenditure items not allowed to be charged thereof as provided in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the relevant guidelines on the utilization of LDRRMF by observing, among others, the following:
Incorporate all identified disaster-related PPAs to be funded by the Municipality in the AIP/SIP; and
Strictly observe the requirements of National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 at all times to ensure that the LDRRMF is used strictly for disaster risk reduction and management activities.
We also reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate transfer of CYs 2016 to 2019 unspent DRRMF from the GF to the STF and thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to properly take up the transfer in the books of the Municipality.
Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the concerned officials to be meticulous in the preparation of LGU reports to ensure that accurate information are provided for decision making.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile their respective Cash-in-Bank records with the depository banks’ within ten days from receipt of the bank statements and with the Treasurer’s cash records at least on a quarterly basis; (b) regularly perform detailed analysis and review of cash accounts; and (c) determine appropriate disposition of reconciling items that have no supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 1.15 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive               direct the  (a) Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, to work on the titling of all lands owned by the Municipality; (b) acting GSO and the Accountant to update and maintain the required PPE records, and reports; and (c) members of the inventory committee, to conduct           a physical count of PPEs in accordance with the prescribed procedures and to render a report thereon in the manner and frequency required by regulations.

Considering that this is a reiteration of the previous years’ audit observation, we recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct reconciliation of the Cash-in-Bank records with the  depository banks within ten days from receipt of the bank statements and with the Treasurer’s cash records at least on a quarterly basis; (b) regularly perform detailed analysis and review of cash accounts; and (c) combine their efforts to retrieve the documents relative the undocumented reconciling items to determine the appropriate action or disposition thereof.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) set-up the RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred Credits accounts at the beginning of the year; and (ii) maintain the appropriate records with the necessary details to support the balances of the receivable and deferred accounts; and (b) Municipal Treasurer at the beginning of every year, prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and furnish the Accounting Office thereof for recording in the books of accounts, in accordance with Sections 19 and 20 of the Manual on New Government Accountting System for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant (a) send demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the liquidation of their accountabilities; (b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones;  (c) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished; and (d) maintain individual Subsidiary Ledgers of all accountable officers for accurate monitoring of their cash advances.

9.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) continuously support and sustain SWM initiatives; and (b) instruct the offices concerned to address the minor lapses noted in planning and implementation of SWM programs, projects and activities.
10.5	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the remittance of all  taxes withheld within the prescribed period as provided in existing BIR regulations.

11.5	We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with existing rules and regulations on deducting and remitting GSIS contributions and loans amortizations in accordance with the GSIS law.
12.3	We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

13.3	We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to remit immediately the unremitted balance of ₱375,776.09 to the HDMF.  Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of PAG-IBIG insurance premiums and due loan amortizations.

14.3	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents before effecting payments to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a)	instruct the GFPS to ensure that (i) the GAD Plan and Budget implemented is DILG approved and (ii) necessary adjustments are reflected and carried out on observations and recommendations by DILG regarding the GPB PPAs for its approval; and (b) comply with the provisions of the aforementioned          circular on the submission of GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant - ICO to locate the documents supporting the transactions booked as CIP for the purpose of ascertaining the propriety of the items comprising the CIP account. Likewise, strict compliance with applicable accounting rules be observed in reclassifying the CIP account to the respective PPE account taking into consideration the status of the projects; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to review pertinent records and conduct ocular inspection to determine the status of the projects recorded in the books under the CIP account whether completed or still ongoing. If completed, provide the certificate of completion and other documents necessary to aid the Municipal Accountant - ICO in the reconciliation of records.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant - ICO to take up the unrecorded disbursements by preparing JEVs duly supported with the paid disbursement vouchers (DVs) and their supporting documents;
Reconstruct the details of unrecorded disbursements and submit the JEVs together with the supporting documents to COA for its audit and verification; and
Ensure that all transactions affecting the CIB accounts are completely and promptly recorded in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the said accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to revisit the unexpended balances of fund transfers received in prior years from various NGAs and thereafter:
Return the balances to the NGAs, or make representations and obtain authority from them to utilize the unexpended balances for purposes other than what are provided in the agreements; and
Request for write-off of the unexpended balances which remained non-moving for 10 years or more and where settlement could no longer be ascertained, in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
We further recommended and Management agreed to:
Fast track the implementation of the projects related to fund transfers since the resources are readily available, and in order not to deprive the general public of the benefits that could be derived therefrom;
Ensure proper accounting and reporting of the utilization of fund transfers received through accurate recording of transactions and proper maintenance of SLs and supporting schedules;
Submit the liquidation reports promptly to SAs in accordance with the terms and conditions stipulated in the MOA or the provisions under COA Circular No. 94-013, in case no specific reportorial requirements are provided in the MOA; and
Conduct periodic reconciliation of its records with the SAs to arrive at a correct/reconciled balance of the Due to NGAs account.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly and intact the trust receipts accruing to the Province and the barangays and comply with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-382 relative to the remittance of the LGU shares from the RPT and SET collections.
We further recommended, and both the Municipal Accountant - ICO and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to exert effort to reconcile their respective records with the barangays on the Due to LGUs - Barangay Share account.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant - ICO to trace the whereabouts of the stale/cancelled checks and thereafter, prepare the necessary adjustments in their respective records duly supported with complete supporting documents; and
Reconstruct the details of stale/cancelled checks and submit the JEVs, checks and supporting documents to COA for its audit and verification.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory and inspection of all unserviceable and obsolete properties so that they may be properly appraised and disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.
We further recommended and Management agreed to require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of these items in order to prevent further deterioration or loss of value.

We recommended and Management agreed to exhaust all possible means to require the liquidation or settlement of cash advances including, but not limited to, the following:
Demand from the concerned AOs the immediate submission of liquidation documents and/or refund of the cash advance if not yet utilized, otherwise, institute appropriate criminal or administrative sanctions for the continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted; and
For long outstanding cash advances, exert effort to retrieve the documents relative thereto and evaluate whether these can be written off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
Also, we reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management strengthen the operation of preventive and monitoring controls to ensure timely submission of liquidation documents and full accounting of balances of cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant - ICO to review their records and effect immediately the necessary adjustments in their respective reports; and
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant - ICO to conduct a periodic reconciliation of their records to come up with a more accurate and reliable reporting on the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Organize an ECMC that will be tasked to prepare the ECM Plan for the Municipality and coordinate the delivery of necessary assistance and services to the evacuees; and
Allocate funds in its LDRRM budget for the construction of additional evacuation centers to address the shortage.
We likewise recommended and the MEO agreed to observe the pertinent provisions of DepEd-DSWD-DILG-DOH JMC No. 1, Series of 2013 and DILG MC No. 122, Series of 2018, in the design and construction of evacuation centers.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) persons responsible for handling and safekeeping of records for the titling of lots to seek legal advise on how to facilitate the titling of those that have no valid documentation; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to deny payments of future lot purchases, unless titled in the name of the Municipality; and (c) OIC-GSO to  (i) to maintain property cards; and (ii) prepare the RPCPPE report and submit the report to the Auditor; (d) Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELC for each category of property to contain the necessary information, and (e) OIC-GSO and Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.
